title: Bridge Essay: Prefrontal contributions to metacognition in perceptual decision making.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.

**Prefrontal contributions to metacognition in perceptual decision making.**. J Neurosci.. PMID: 22553018. DOI: 10.1523/JNEUROSCI.6489-11.2012. Neuroscience has made considerable progress in understanding the neural substrates supporting cognitive performance in anumber of domains, including memory, perception, and decision making. In contrast, how the human brain generates metacognitive awareness of task performance remains unclear. Here, we address this question by asking participants to perform perceptual decisions while providing concurrent metacognitive reports during fMRI scanning.
